communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019540-050526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
06/03/2026 Miriam Bhimani 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019540-050526 Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

An email log was released in FOIA N019423-041326. 

Supply highlighted emails (attached) from the email log supplied in N019423-041326. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Attachments: 

https://cdn.muckrock.com/outbound_composer_attachments/miriamcb/176619/FOIA_19423_Redacted_Highlights.xlsx 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2281470/ RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that certain private information was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. Student/family information has been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). Section 7(1)(f) exempts from release “Preliminary drafts, notes, recommendations, memoranda and other records in which opinions are expressed, or policies or actions are formulated, except that a specific record or relevant portion of a record shall not be exempt when the record is publicly cited and identified by the head of the public body.” [5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(f)]. Section 7(1)(n) exempts records relating to a public body's adjudication of employee disciplinary cases. Section 7.5(h) exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). Materials subject to attorney-client privilege and work-product privilege have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(m) which exempts communications between a public body and an attorney representing the public body that would not be subject to discovery in litigation, and materials prepared or compiled by or for a public body in anticipation of a criminal, civil or administrative proceeding upon the request of an attorney advising the public body. Communications between a public body and an attorney or auditor representing the public body that would not be subject to discovery in litigation, and materials prepared or compiled by or for a public body in anticipation of a criminal, civil or administrative proceeding upon the request of an attorney advising the public body, and materials prepared or compiled with respect to internal audits of public bodies. You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Department of Defense support for 2024 "Coming Home" music video by Old Dominion

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for 2024 "Coming Home" music video by Old Dominion
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the 2024 music video "Coming Home" by the musical act Old Dominion. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Coming Home," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Coming Home Music Video - Old Dominion" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Police disciplinary files
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States.
- Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aspen

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P050972-073025
communications.communication:
Dear Aspen: 
 
Your request has been logged in WebQA and has been given the reference number P050972-073025 for tracking purposes. 
 
Clark County should receive your request today, except if today is a holiday or weekend, in which case receipt will be on the next business day.  You should receive a response from Clark County within five business days of their receipt that: (1) provides records; (2) provides an internet address allowing you to access records; (3) acknowledges your request and provides a reasonable estimate of the time required to respond to the request; or (4) denies the request. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below. 
 
Clark County, WA

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P050972-073025
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Dear Aspen Aspen: In accordance with the Public Records Act, RCW 42.56.520, this letter acknowledges receipt of your PRA request submitted to and received by the Clark County Prosecutor’s Office on 7/30/2025.  
This request has been assigned tracking number P050972-073025 .  Please use this number in any communications concerning this request. Specifically, you requested: - Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States. 
- Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers. 
We have begun a process to search for records responsive to your request.  If we discover the records you requested, we will review them for applicable exemptions from disclosure and make them available to you.  If we do not discover any records responsive to your request, we will inform you.  If necessary, we may also inform you that we have notified third persons or agencies of their right to seek a protective order before releasing any documents responsive to your request. We may also ask you for additional clarifications if your request is unclear after a review of the documents we have or don’t have. Based upon other pending requests and availability of personnel, at this writing we can reasonably estimate that a response to your request will be available on or before September 17, 2025.  If the records become available in advance of this estimate, we will contact you promptly.  Conversely, if we encounter a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you with a revised estimated date. RCW 42.56.120 provides that the County may charge fees as authorized by law for responsive public records. For additional explanation of public disclosure regulations, please visit the Washington State Attorney General’s public records page at http://www.atg.wa.gov/obtaining-records If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me. Kind regards, Mindy Lamberton 
Public Records Clark County Prosecutor's Office

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P050972-073025
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Good afternoon, 
Thank you for your email.  Please see my email dated 9-16-25. 
You requested "any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers".  To provide you with some context regarding the scope of your request: 
For each individual listed on the PID lists there is a dedicated folder containing their PID materials, which are responsive to your request.  These materials are EXTENSIVE and include reports, audio files, jail records, personnel records, body worn camera footage etc.  Our office requests all investigative materials collected by the agency for the investigation to be sent to our office for review.   All records must be carefully reviewed for applicable exemptions under the PRA prior to release.  Given the breadth of content, fulfilling this request will take several months. I had indicated a first installment would be available on or before November 5, 2025, with continuing installments until your request is fulfilled. **If you'd like to narrow the scope of your request to just the findings for the incident for each deputy/officer on the PID list, that could help considerably. 
Please let me know your thoughts.  Otherwise, I will have an installment available for you on November 5, 2025. 
Kind regards, 
Mindy Lamberton 
Public Records

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P050972-073025
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Re:       Your Public Records Request, Tracking Number P050972-073025  Dear Aspen Aspen:  Uploaded to the portal you will find the 1st installment of records responsive to your request, Deputy Chris Anderson's PID materials. Please note that some information has been redacted from the records pursuant to the Public Records Act.  The exemption log will explain and provide the statutory language for the redactions. We reasonably believe that a 2nd installment of public records responsive to this request will be available on or before December 10, 2025 .  If public records become available in advance of this estimate, we will contact you promptly.  Conversely, if this office encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you with a revised estimated date.  We will communicate with you and keep you informed of any updates regarding collecting and obtaining the responsive records as we move through the process. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me. Kind regards, Mindy Lamberton Public Records Clark County Prosecutor's Office

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me. Kind regards, Mindy Lamberton Public Records Clark County Prosecutor's Office

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P050972-073025
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Dear Aspen Aspen:  This communication is in regard to your Public Records Request, P050972-073025 . On December 11, 2025, we contacted you by email to let you know that the 2nd installment of records responsive to your request was available for downloading or review.  To date we have not heard from you, nor have the records been downloaded or reviewed.  Per RCW 42.56.120(4) “ If an installment of a records request is not claimed or reviewed, the agency is not obligated to fulfill the balance of the request.” Therefore, this request is considered abandoned and is now closed. Please be aware that there is a one-year statute of limitations for judicial review of this request, and that has started to run as of the date of this letter because our agency does not intend to further address this request. To open a new public records request, please go to the on-line public records portal at https://clark.wa.gov/councilors/public-records. Kind regards, Mindy Lamberton Public Records Clark County Prosecutor's Office

title: Chicago Police Department FOIA Requests Between January 1, 2025, and September 30, 2025

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Chicago Police Department FOIA Requests Between January 1, 2025, and September 30, 2025
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

I would like to request a log of all requests to the Chicago Police Department between January 1, 2025, and September 30, 2025.

I would like to receive the file number for each request, name of the person or organization making the request, the file number for each request, the date the request was received, date the agency responded and a description of the information sought and the nature of all responses.

I request a waiver on all potential fees as this is for an academic project and will not be used for commercial purposes.

Please send the log request to the listed email.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tyler Bednar

communications.subject: Welcome to the City of Chicago Request Center!
communications.communication:

Thank you for registering with the City of Chicago Public Records Portal. 
If you did not submit your request through the public portal but are receiving this e-mail, we are excited to share with you that you can now submit FOIA requests through a newly implemented online request system. communications.communication:
--- --- 

10/08/2025 
HICAGO OLICE EPARTMENT 
CITY OF CHICAGO 
Tyler Bednar 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR194357 263 Huntington Ave 
Boston MA 02115 
RE: FOIA REQUEST of October 07, 2025, Reference # P106289-100725 
Dear Tyler : 
The Chicago Police Department is in receipt of your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request. On October 07, 2025, the following request was received. To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

I would like to request a log of all requests to the Chicago Police Department between January 1, 2025, and September 30, 2025. 

I would like to receive the file number for each request, name of the person or organization making the request, the file number for each request, the date the request was received, date the agency responded and a description of the information sought and the nature of all responses. 

I request a waiver on all potential fees as this is for an academic project and will not be used for commercial purposes. 

Please send the log request to the listed email. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Tyler Bednar 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2103162/ 
Under the Freedom of Information Act, a public body may extend the time to respond to a FOIA request by up to 5 business days for a limited number of reasons. 5 ILCS 140/3(e). We are extending the time to respond to your request by 5 business days for the following reason(s): 
The request may require the collection of a substantial number of specified records, if any exist; 
The requested records, if any exist, may require examination and evaluation by personnel having the necessary competence and discretion to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of the Freedom of Information Act or should be 
If you have any questions, please contact our office at 312-745-5308 or at the following address: Chicago Police Department Attention: Freedom of Information Officer Office of Legal Affairs, Unit 114 3510 S. Michigan Ave. Chicago, IL 60653 
Sincerely, Robert Earnshaw 
Freedom of Information Officer 
Chicago Police Department

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: NIH Grant No. 1R37CA300386
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Project Number: 1R37CA300386
Project Title: A Single, Universal, and Digitally Programmable RF System Enables MRI of Any Nucleus
Principal Investigator: HAN, VICTOR 

Program Official: ZHANG, HUIMING
Opportunity Number: PA-25-301
Organization: UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA BERKELEY
NIH Institute or Center: National Cancer Institute
Project Start Date: 01-April-2026

Specifically, I request the following records, to the extent they are available and releasable:
1. The funded grant application in its final submitted form.
2. The abstract, specific aims, research strategy, budget pages, and other components included in the awarded application.
3. Any revised or resubmitted funded version of the application, if applicable.
4. All funded progress reports for the award, including each annual Research Performance Progress Report (RPPR), any multi-year funded progress reports if applicable, and the final RPPR or other closeout progress report, to the extent maintained by NIH and releasable under FOIA.
5. Any other grant application materials maintained by NIH that are part of the awarded application file and are subject to release under FOIA.

This request is limited to existing agency records. I am not requesting the creation of new records, explanations, analyses, or responses to questions.
If any portion of the requested material is exempt from disclosure, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions. For each withheld portion, please identify the basis for withholding, including the applicable FOIA exemption(s).


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Sterman

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Fall River Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Fall River Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

communications.subject: FOIA
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Finch,


Thank you for contacting The Jackson County Sheriff’s Office with your request for information, pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act - 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq.

          I received your written request for information through the Freedom of Information Act on Wednesday, May 20, 2026, requesting the following information: (See full attached request).

“I hereby request the following records:
Electronic copies of all body-worn camera and dashboard camera recordings related to the case indicated, including all footage from the primary officer as well as any responding officers.”

          Under the Freedom of Information Act, a public body may extend the time to respond to a FOIA request by up to 5 business days for a limited number of reasons. 5 ILCS140/3(e).  We are extending the time to respond to your request by 5 business days for the following reason(s).

          The information, you requested, is being evaluated by trained personnel to ensure proper redactions are made.  If you have any question(s) please feel free to call me. (618) 684-2177.

          You have the right to have this response to your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). That contact information is:


    Public Access Counselor
                                        Office of the Attorney General
                                        500 S. 2nd St.
                                        Springfield, IL 62701                                                                                                  Phone:1-877-299-FOIA (3642)
                                        Fax: 217-782-1396
                                        E-mail: publicaccess@ilag.gov<mailto:publicaccess@ilag.gov>

You also have the right to seek judicial review of this response by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11.

  If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within sixty (60) calendar days of the date of response. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC.


Respectfully,




Shauna Taylor- Patrol Lieutenant

Phone: 618-684-2177  Email: shauna.taylor@jacksoncounty-il.gov

Jackson County Sheriff's Office –

1001 Mulberry Street, Murphysboro, IL 62966

title: Mefloquine Active Comparator Adverse Event Data Reported in NDA 21-078 Application

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act.  I hereby request the following records:

Records of adverse events reported in the mefloquine comparator arms of all active-controlled clinical studies submitted in support of the indication for “prevention of malaria” for NDA 21-078 (Malarone) tablets or pediatric tablets, for example, those reported in Table 4 of the current Malarone prescribing information, to specifically include any raw, free-text, or subject-level data on such adverse events.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that fees cannot be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dr. Remington Nevin
An acknowledgement letter, stating the request is being processed.

To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Freedom of Information request, copied below, and originally submitted on Oct. 5, 2015. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response, or if further clarification is needed.  You had assigned it reference number #2015-8145.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Status inquiry 2015-8145 MALARONE
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Nevin,

In response to your request for a status update for your request related to MALARONE NDA 21-078 - AER please note, the FDA CDER processes FOI requests in a two track system. That system operates on a first in first out basis. Your request has been assigned to the complex queue. Cases assigned to the complex queue generally experience a processing time frame of 18 to 24 months. Your request is in the queue, and will be processed in turn.
Regards,
Gary Jackson
Consumer Safety Officer

Office of Regulatory Policy
Division of Information Disclosure Policy
Center for Drug Evaluation and Research

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Request #2015-8145
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Freedom of Information request, copied below, and originally submitted on Oct. 5, 2015. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response, or if further clarification is needed.  You had assigned it reference number #2015-8145.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

Headquarters FOIA Officer
Food and Drug Administration
Division of Freedom of Information
5630 Fishers Lane, Room 1035
Rockville, MD 20857

Re: Appeal of Constructive Denial of Freedom of Information Act Request FDA 2015-8145

Dear Sir or Madam,

This is an appeal under the Freedom of Information Act. 

On October 5, 2015, I submitted a request for:

"Records of adverse events reported in the mefloquine comparator arms of all active-controlled clinical studies submitted in support of the indication for 'prevention of malaria' for NDA 21-078 (Malarone) tablets or pediatric tablets, for example, those reported in Table 4 of the current Malarone prescribing information, to specifically include any raw, free-text, or subject-level data on such adverse events."

This request was acknowledged by FDA in a letter dated October 7, 2015. In a message dated November 9, 2015, FDA informed me that the request was in its complex queue, with an expected processing time of 18-24 months. It has now been nearly 24 months, and despite monthly requests for an update on the status of this request, I have received no further response or correspondence from FDA. 

The FDA's failure to respond to my request within the time period required by the Freedom of Information Act serves as a constructive denial of my request.

I respectfully request that FDA respond to my appeal within 20 business days. 

Sincerely, 

Dr. Remington Nevin

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2015-8145
communications.communication:
Good morning,

Regarding your request for processing status please note that the FDA CDER processes FOI requests in a two track system. That system operates on a first in first out basis. Your request has been assigned to the complex queue. Cases assigned to the complex queue generally experience a processing time frame of approximately 18 to 24 months. however, in light of the many COVID-19 requests that CDER has received and which have been granted expedited processing – which moves them up the queue ahead of requests that came in before them – we cannot provide a definitive response timeframe. Your request is in the queue, and will be processed in turn.

Thank you for your understanding and patience.
Regards,
Gary Jackson
Consumer Safety Officer
Division of Information Disclosure Policy
Office of Regulatory Policy
Center for Drug Evaluation and Research
U.S. Food and Drug Administration

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Ortega

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P000525-052026
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: Police Records Request of May 20, 2026, Reference # P000525-052026 Dear Daniel Ortega, The LaGrange Police Department received a public records request from you on May 20, 2026. Your request mentioned: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. Organization Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency. 

2. Network Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System. 

3. Event Log in Flock 
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Daniel Ortega The LaGrange Police Department has reviewed its files and has located responsive records to your request. Please log in to the Records Request Center at the following link to retrieve the responsive records. Please note that records only go back 31 days. Flock permanently deletes all records older than that. Police Records Request - P000525-052026  For questions or additional information, please reply to this email. Regards, LaGrange Police Department

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Edward Nixon
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records relating to, mentioning, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into Edward Calvert Nixon (May 3, 1930 - February 27, 2019). Proof of death is not required as his death was widely reported: https://www.cnn.com/2019/02/27/us/edward-nixon-dies/index.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. This search is also to include any responsive records held in offsite locations, such as the Alexandria Records Center. Please include both investigatory and non-investigatory records in your search. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante

Supporting documentation **

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

** 

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1553432-000
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.  Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on "Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request" on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI's FOIPA Program is searching the FBI's indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 98 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 947 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 2,011 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 2,360 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Transfer/Transport of Captive-held  Marine Mammals
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Under the provisions of the Freedom of Information Act 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request all permit applications concerning the transfer/transport of captive-held marine mammals per the Marine Mammal Protection Act (P.L. 92-522) submitted from January l, 2019 to the present.

I respectfully request a waiver of all fees under 5 U.S.C. Section 552(a) (4) (A) (iii). As an academic researcher, I will actively share and provide access to these records with the public and my colleagues. Release of these materials is in the public interest, and will greatly contribute to public knowledge of U.S. Fish and Wildlife activities and formation of policy. I am happy to receive any and all records in electronic format. Please note that 5 U.S.C. Section 552(a) (4) (A) (iv) (II) requires that you provide the first 100 copies to me at no charge.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Susan Maret

communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse: 
Thank you for your interest in the public records of the Washington State Military Department. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 4/22/2026 and given the reference number P001910-042226 for tracking purposes. 
We are currently in the process of researching the documents potentially responsive to your request. You may expect an Installment or update regarding the status of your request within 2 weeks of this submittal.  PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, do legal research, or answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request in "My Request Center".  You'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Again, thank you for using the Public Records Portal. This is an auto-generated email and has originated from an unmonitored

Dear Rose Terse, 
The Washington State Military Department received a public information request from you on April 22, 2026. Your request mentioned: 
“To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All emails between Caroline.Byrd@mil.wa.gov and @dataminr.com email addresses between August 1, 2025 and October 1, 2025, including attachments and associated metadata. 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2272282/” 
The Washington State Military Department has reviewed its files and has located responsive records to your request. Under the authority of RCW 42.56.120(2)(b) and WAC 323-10-070, the Department now charges fees for providing copies of public records. 

The cost for these records is as follows: 

Fees Charged: Five cents per each four electronic files or attachment uploaded to email, cloud-based data storage service, or other means of electronic delivery. 

The Department has estimated that the the expenses of processing payment exceeds the costs of providing these records. Therefore the fees for these records have been waived. 

Please log in to the Public Records Portal at the following link to retrieve the appropriate responsive documents. Public Records Request - P001910-042226 
Records containing redactions should be read to the last page of each document for an explanation of the information withheld from disclosure under the authority of RCW 42.56. 

If you disagree with any denial of records under your request, you may petition for internal review under RCW 42.56.520, within ten business days. You may request a review in writing by mail, fax, or email via: 

Cynthia Whaley, Public Records Officer 
Washington Military Department 
Building 1 
1 Militia Drive 
Camp Murray, WA 98430 
E-mail: public.records@mil.wa.gov 
This production of records is a complete response to your request. Your request has been fulfilled by the production of records (including any exemptions claimed) and is now closed. This is our final response. The WA Military Department does not intend to further address the request, and the PRA's one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run as of the date of this email. Please contact me within 30 days by e-mail at public.records@mil.wa.gov or at Building One, 1 Militia Drive, Camp Murray, WA 98430-5000, if you have any questions. 
Sincerely,

title: Subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Surprise AZ Planning & Zoning (Community Development)

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Surprise AZ Planning & Zoning (Community Development)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

To the Public Records/FOIA Officer:
Under the Arizona Public Records Act, I am requesting an opportunity to obtain copies of public records relating to the property located at 13290 W Sweetwater Ave, Surprise, AZ 85379. 
Specifically, I am requesting the following records created, submitted, or received for the Surprise Pointe Commerce Center, as well as any of these records between January 1, 2025 and the date this request is processed related to retrofitting the Surprise Pointe Commerce Center. 

This request is similar to 'City Records Request :: R000999-021926' made on February 19th, 2026.

- All building permits, renovation permits, variance requests, or "Change of Use" applications filed for the aforementioned property.
- All code compliance inspection reports, certificates of occupancy, and violation notices regarding the property.
- Any site plans, architectural drawings, or engineering evaluations submitted by the property owner, their LLC, or the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) regarding retrofitting the facility.

To expedite this request and reduce the administrative burden on your office, I am requesting these records in their native electronic format (e.g., PDF for documents, .PST or .EML for emails) delivered via email or secure download link.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. 

Thank you for your time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gavin Laughland

Specifically, I am requesting the following records created, submitted, or received for the Surprise Pointe Commerce Center, as well as any of these records between January 1, 2025 and the date this request is processed related to retrofitting the Surprise Pointe Commerce Center. 

This request is similar to 'City Records Request :: R000999-021926' made on February 19th, 2026. 

- All building permits, renovation permits, variance requests, or "Change of Use" applications filed for the aforementioned property. 
- All code compliance inspection reports, certificates of occupancy, and violation notices regarding the property. 
- Any site plans, architectural drawings, or engineering evaluations submitted by the property owner, their LLC, or the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) regarding retrofitting the facility. 

To expedite this request and reduce the administrative burden on your office, I am requesting these records in their native electronic format (e.g., PDF for documents, .PST or .EML for emails) delivered via email or secure download link. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. 

Thank you for your time. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days. 

Sincerely, 

Gavin Laughland 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2281082/

title: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 23, 2026

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 23, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of the minutes for the Planning Board meeting held on March 23, 2026.

Please provide any minutes for this meeting, whether in draft or final form.

If no minutes exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

This request includes any records that document the discussions, deliberations, and actions taken at the March 23, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

For context, I note that the Planning Board subsequently met on April 6, April 27, May 4, and May 18, 2026. At the May 18, 2026 meeting, the Board approved the March 9, 2026 meeting minutes.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eileen Kelleher

communications.subject: PRR 2026-53 RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 23, 2026
communications.communication:
This office is in receipt of your request for public records information. We will be in contact with you if any changes are incurred when filling your request. Please be advised we have 10 business days to respond to your request or notify you that more time will be needed and any applicable fees.

Please feel free to contact us if you require any further assistance.

Thank you, Amy

communications.subject: RE: PRR 2026-53 RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 23, 2026
communications.communication:
Please see the attached requested Planning Board Draft minutes for the meeting held on March 26, 2026.

You may appeal this response to the Supervisor of Public Records pursuant to 950 CMR 32.08(1)(d). By law, the Supervisor is required to respond within 10 business days of receipt of your appeal. You may also seek judicial review of an unfavorable response by commencing a civil action in the superior court, under G.L. c. 66, § 10A(c).

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: County and Municipal Budgets
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All county and municipal budgets from year 2012-2013 to 2025-2026 for each county and municipal government as listed on the State Auditor’s website at https://www.wvsao.gov/LocalGovernment/Default. I’m requesting this data because there is not a bulk download button on the website, and the process of gathering and sending the data to me should be more simple for you than for me to individually download or scrape your website. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. On the State Auditor’s website, these files are provided as PDFs. However, I suspect that the raw data is stored in a tabular data format like spreadsheets or a relational database. If this the case, please note that I am requesting the tabular or native digital format and not the PDF reports. If there you are not in possession of any responsive records other than the PDFs, then I am requesting the PDFs themselves. 

If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: FOIA 2492 Flock Cameras
communications.communication:
Good evening,

Please accept this letter as notification for extension regarding the FOIA request you have submitted regarding " Flock Cameras."

      Section 5(2)(d) of the FOIA statue allows a public body to extend for not more than ten (10) business days the period during which a public body shall respond to a request.

This public body, pursuant to the statute referred above, herby gives notice to you that it is extending the period of time for which it may respond to your request ten (10) business days. Accordingly, the period this public may respond to your request shall be on or before the 1st day of June, 2026.


Best regards,


Erika Mazzitello, FOIA Coordinator

communications.subject: Re: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

The Dearborn Heights Police Department has received your request for public records and has processed it pursuant to the provisions of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), ML 15.231 et seq.

Your request is approved in part and denied in part. (The links were sent in a separate email.)
DENIED- January 1, 2023 to present. The FLOCK system only allows the past 30 days.

There is no amount due.

In the event that you are not satisfied with this response, you have the right to submit a written appeal to the head of this public body, Mayor Bill Bazzi. It must specifically state the word “appeal” and identify the reason or reasons for reversal of this denial by myself. In addition, you may also seek judicial review of this denial pursuant to MCL 15.240. If you seek judicial review and the Court determines the public body made an improper decision regarding the records sought, then you may be entitled to attorney fees and the potential damages set forth in the statute.


Regards,

Erika Mazzitello, FOIA Coordinator

title: FBI support for TV documentary "The Case Of: Caylee Anthony" (2018)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI support for TV documentary "The Case Of: Caylee Anthony" (2018)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to FBI support for or collaboration with the production of the Oxygen Network TV documentary "The Case Of: Caylee Anthony" (2018), directed by Donnie Eichar. Such documents may include, but should not be limited to, script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the FBI and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "The Case Of: Caylee Anthony," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication. I request a search of all locations where such documents might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ or similar offices.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: William M. Beecher (May 27, 1933 – February 9, 2024)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into investigative journalist William M. Beecher (May 27, 1933 – February 9, 2024). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2024/02/18/business/media/william-beecher-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an investigative journalist who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, Reason Magazine, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1620220-000
communications.communication:
To whom this may concern,

My name is Matthew Petti and I am writing in response to my FOIA request number 1620220-000. Thank you so much for your prompt response.

In your letter dated 6 March 2024, you write that "additional records potentially responsive to your subject may exist. If this release of previously processed material does not satisfy your request, you may request an additional search for records."

I would like to formally advise that I would like an additional search for records. Thank you again for your prompt attention to this matter. If possible, please let me know if you've received this message.

Best regards,
Matthew

communications.subject: FOIA Request 1620220-001/William Beecher
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

We're contacting you in reference to the FOIPA request number included in the subject line of this email.  The FBI located approximately 2,083 pages potentially responsive to your request. 

Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system:
Extra-small track requests (0-50 pages) current average time is approximately 8 months to complete;
Small track requests (51-250 pages) current average time is approximately 36 months to complete;
Medium track requests (251-500 pages) current average time is approximately 44 months to complete;
Large track requests (501-4,999 pages) current average time is approximately 67 months to complete; and
Extra-large track requests (5,000+ pages) current average time is approximately 79 months to complete.

The current average time to complete your large track request is at least 67 months. Reducing the scope of your request may accelerate the processing, allow for a timelier receipt of the information you seek, and reduce the duplication costs, if applicable.  Would you be willing to consider reducing the scope of your request to place it in a smaller, potentially faster processing track?

Please let us know if this is something you would be interested in further discussing. If you chose to narrow the scope of your request, you will be able to request remaining material via a new FOIPA request submission at the completion of this request, subject to applicable fees.

Respectfully,

Negotiation Team
Federal Bureau of Investigation
Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov<mailto:Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov>

Status Checks: https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__vault.fbi.gov_fdps-2D1_-40-40search-2Dfdps&d=DwMFAw&c=sJ6xIWYx-zLMB3EPkvcnVg&r=XBMMkObpUV4qgiRWe16ruf84MlE_zzfeACVcd84hmls&m=Aw62oYIKH07oNuehHecdHgLWMqlWFG0DYoQjNpzMHkhbi3BtoiHrci3yBRh212Wp&s=SdHEKbKopxbZbSeRluCcd86nmwJXks1SzF_CE3LxaTE&e=> | foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>
For additional information, please visit the www.fbi.gov/foia<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__www.fbi.gov_foia&d=DwMFAw&c=sJ6xIWYx-zLMB3EPkvcnVg&r=XBMMkObpUV4qgiRWe16ruf84MlE_zzfeACVcd84hmls&m=Aw62oYIKH07oNuehHecdHgLWMqlWFG0DYoQjNpzMHkhbi3BtoiHrci3yBRh212Wp&s=-aTlmvcM5Bk6Q7nyu9yDBhN3v2HmU3sGIZ7u9r2JLFg&e=> website.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1620220-001
communications.communication:
Dear Negotiation Team,

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Matthew Petti and I am writing with regard to my FOIA request number 1620220-001/William Beecher. Do you have any suggestions on how to reduce the scope of the request? Otherwise, I am happy to leave it in the large track.

Sincerely,
Matthew

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1620220-001
communications.communication:
Good morning,
Thank you for your email concerning the scope reduction of this request.  Unfortunately, we are limited on what we can discuss since the potentially responsive pages haven’t been reviewed by a FOIA analyst.
After further review, the potentially responsive pages are within the date range of 1953-1987.
We recommend the following options:

We could narrow the scope of this request by limiting to a specific date range within 1953-1987.

We could reduce the scope of this request by limiting to approximately 50 potentially responsive pages.  This request would move to the simple track with an average time to complete of approximately 8 months.

We could reduce the scope of this request by limiting to approximately 250 potentially responsive pages.  This request would move to the small track with an average time to complete of approximately 36 months.

We could reduce the scope of this request by limiting to approximately 500 potentially responsive pages.  This request would move to the medium track with an average time to complete of approximately 44 months.

Please let us know how you would like to move forward with reducing the scope of your request.
Respectfully,

Negotiation Team
Federal Bureau of Investigation
Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov<mailto:Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov>

Status Checks: https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__vault.fbi.gov_fdps-2D1_-40-40search-2Dfdps&d=DwMFAw&c=sJ6xIWYx-zLMB3EPkvcnVg&r=XBMMkObpUV4qgiRWe16ruf84MlE_zzfeACVcd84hmls&m=Aw62oYIKH07oNuehHecdHgLWMqlWFG0DYoQjNpzMHkhbi3BtoiHrci3yBRh212Wp&s=SdHEKbKopxbZbSeRluCcd86nmwJXks1SzF_CE3LxaTE&e=> | foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

title: Amtrak Board of Directors - Meeting Agendas | May 2026

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Amtrak Board of Directors - Meeting Agendas | May 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The agenda for any scheduled meeting of the Amtrak Board of Directors held during the month of May 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ben Heckscher

communications.subject: Acknowledgement - 26-FOI-00503
communications.communication:
June 1, 2026



Ben Heckscher

N/A

MuckRock News, DEPT MR212300, 263 Huntington Ave

Boston, MA  02115



Dear Mr. Heckscher:



This letter acknowledges the receipt of your FOIA request dated May 31, 2026 requesting To Whom It May Concern: Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records: The agenda for any scheduled meeting of the Amtrak Board of Directors held during the month of May 2026. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires. communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: PD Settlement Agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

 A copy of every agreement to settle a lawsuit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim against this agency over the past 10 years. This covers all agreements that would fall under New Hampshire RSA 91-A:4 (Right-to-Know Law), VI:

VI. Every agreement to settle a lawsuit against a governmental unit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim, entered into by any political subdivision or its insurer, shall be kept on file at the municipal clerk's office and made available for public inspection for a period of no less than 10 years from the date of settlement.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.communication:
To the FOIA Officer of Skokie Police Department,

This request is made under Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

1. Any Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request or other records request received by your agency from the Office of the Illinois Secretary of State concerning Automatic License Plate Reader (ALPR) systems or 625 ILCS 5/2-130, from January 1, 2025 to the date of this request.

A response is anticipated within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs
